title: Start Adopting AI in Your Accounting Practice
description: Go from "I know I should use AI" to actually using it daily — with a practical, low-risk approach any accountant can follow. Based on Brock Beyer's advice for accountants who haven't started yet.
globalInfo: Source: Brock Beyer — LinkedIn post (2026-04-18 scrape). Core message: stop overthinking tool choice and just start. Apply a rules-based vs. judgment-based filter to know what to automate. Ask AI to help you learn AI. Keep authenticity where it matters.

modules.title: From Zero to Daily AI User

steps.title: Just Start — Pick Any AI Tool and Open It
steps.content: Don't overthink the tool choice. Open Claude, ChatGPT, or any LLM. The biggest barrier isn't picking the right tool — it's not starting at all. Open a free account and type your first question today.

steps.title: Ask the AI a Real Accounting Question
steps.content: Instead of searching Stack Exchange or the FASB codification, ask the AI. Try something like: "What's the correct treatment for a prepaid expense that spans two fiscal years?" Compare the answer to what you know. This builds trust (or healthy skepticism).

steps.title: Fail Fast, Fail Early — Learn from Mistakes
steps.content: Your first AI outputs will have errors. That's expected. The goal is to learn what it gets right, what it gets wrong, and how to refine your prompts. Each mistake teaches you how to work with the tool more effectively.

steps.title: Apply the Rules-Based vs. Judgment-Based Filter
steps.content: Before automating anything, ask: "Is this task rules-based or does it require professional judgment?" Rules-based tasks (data entry, categorization, reconciliation) are ideal for AI. Judgment-heavy tasks (materiality decisions, going concern assessments) should stay human — use AI as a thought partner, not the decision-maker.

steps.title: When You're Stuck, Ask the AI for Help with AI
steps.content: Meta, but powerful. If you don't know how to prompt effectively, ask: "I'm an accountant trying to use AI to automate [task]. Can you help me write a good prompt for this?" The AI will coach you on how to use it better.

steps.title: Keep Authenticity Where It Matters
steps.content: Not everything should be AI-generated. Client communications, advisory conversations, and relationship-building should retain your voice. Use AI for the grunt work so you have more time for the human work.
